context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements Turbo Stream responses for controller actions, creating the right view templates, using respond_to with format.turbo_stream, and using the correct turbo_stream helper actions for DOM manipulation.

checklist.name: respond_to turbo_stream
checklist.description: The controller action includes a respond_to block that contains format.turbo_stream (in addition to format.html)

checklist.name: create stream template
checklist.description: A .turbo_stream.erb view template file is created for the create (or relevant) action (e.g. create.turbo_stream.erb)

checklist.name: update stream template
checklist.description: A .turbo_stream.erb view template file is created for the update action

checklist.name: destroy stream template
checklist.description: A .turbo_stream.erb view template file is created for the destroy action

checklist.name: turbo_stream append
checklist.description: Uses turbo_stream.append (or turbo_stream.prepend) in the create stream template to add a new item to a DOM list

checklist.name: turbo_stream replace
checklist.description: Uses turbo_stream.replace in the update stream template to replace an existing DOM element

checklist.name: turbo_stream remove
checklist.description: Uses turbo_stream.remove in the destroy stream template to remove a DOM element

checklist.name: turbo_frame wraps list
checklist.description: A turbo_frame_tag wraps the list/container region (not the entire page layout), giving the Turbo Stream targets a DOM anchor

checklist.name: HTML fallback present
checklist.description: The format.html branch in respond_to redirects or renders (i.e., a fallback exists for non-Turbo requests)

checklist.name: JS-disabled behavior noted
checklist.description: The implementation notes, comments, or a README section confirms the feature works with JavaScript disabled (form submission still completes, list updates via full page load)

checklist.name: No Turbo Frame for navigation
checklist.description: turbo_frame_tag is NOT used to wrap site-wide navigation elements (header, nav bar, sidebar) or to drive page-to-page transitions
